mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) signal strengthening consumer demand and corporate earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism operates through improved profit expectations without requiring monetary accommodation.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 343623M as of May 2005 reflects sustained consumer spending
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS momentum and 20-day SPX returns falls below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive RSAFS growth fails to produce positive 20d returns in > 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 3.0% and continues rising, the Fed is in active tightening mode. This compresses equity valuations by raising the risk-free discount rate and signals future economic slowdown, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as markets front-run growth deceleration.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 3.0% as of May 2005 marks the threshold of restrictive policy
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 3.0% and rising, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating payroll employment (PAYEMS) combined with stable unemployment (UNRATE) indicates tight labor markets and wage pressure, which constrains corporate margins and signals Fed will maintain or tighten policy. This produces negative 20-day SPX returns as profit growth expectations decline.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 133347k and UNRATE at 5.1% as of May 2005 show tight labor market
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with wage inflation risk
failure_condition: 20-day SPX returns are positive in > 55% of triggered instances despite tight labor conditions, or the joint effect of PAYEMS and UNRATE shows Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines month-over-month while remaining above trend, signaling a slowdown in manufacturing capacity utilization. This precedes broader economic deceleration and equity market weakness, producing negative 60-day SPX returns as forward earnings estimates are revised downward.
failure_condition: 60-day SPX returns are positive in > 58% of instances following INDPRO declines, or Rank IC between INDPRO momentum and 60-day returns remains below 0.02 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) both accelerate simultaneously, indicating broad-based demand strength across residential and consumer sectors. This joint signal predicts sustained earnings growth and positive 60-day SPX returns, with the interaction capturing non-linear amplification of growth expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 2038k and RSAFS at 343623M as of April-May 2005
regime_hint: mid-cycle expansion with synchronized demand growth
failure_condition: 60-day SPX returns are negative in > 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction term (HOUST × RSAFS momentum) shows Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates above 2.5% year-over-year while the federal funds rate remains below 3.5%, creating a negative real rate environment. This signals monetary policy is accommodative relative to inflation, supporting equity valuations and producing positive 20-day SPX returns as real asset demand increases.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 194.1 and FEDFUNDS at 3.0% as of May 2005
regime_hint: accommodative monetary policy with moderate inflation
failure_condition: 20-day SPX returns are negative in > 55% of triggered instances, or the real rate interaction shows Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month period, signaling labor market deterioration and reduced consumer confidence. This triggers a negative feedback loop where lower employment growth reduces aggregate demand, producing negative 60-day SPX returns as recession risk increases.
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or early recession
failure_condition: 60-day SPX returns are positive in > 60% of instances following UNRATE increases > 0.3pp, or Rank IC between UNRATE changes and 60-day returns falls below 0.02 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates to below 2.5% annualized rate while remaining positive, indicating late-cycle slowdown without recession. This signals peak earnings growth has passed and equity valuations should compress, producing negative 20-day SPX returns as forward guidance becomes more cautious.
failure_condition: 20-day SPX returns are positive in > 58% of instances when GDPC1 growth < 2.5%, or Rank IC between GDPC1 growth and 20-day returns remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.
